audit_items.proof_trace: All three public functions (ldap_t61s_valid, ldap_t61s_to_utf8s, ldap_utf8s_to_t61s) dereference their struct berval* arguments at their first line of code with no prior null check, so passing NULL for str/src/dst causes an immediate null pointer dereference. Proof of safety fails at input validation — none of the functions guard against NULL berval pointers or NULL bv_val fields when bv_len > 0. However, exhaustive grep across the entire repository finds zero callers of these functions, making them effectively dead code in the current codebase; reachability from external LDAP input is not established.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An external application or module that links against libldap and calls ldap_t61s_valid, ldap_t61s_to_utf8s, or ldap_utf8s_to_t61s with a NULL berval pointer (or a berval with bv_len > 0 and bv_val == NULL) would trigger a null pointer dereference and crash; within the audited slapd codebase no such call path exists.
